bio: Kevin Muniz is a software engineer and graduate student pursuing a Master of Science in Data Science at the University of Texas at Austin, with an expected graduation of December 2027. He completed his Bachelor of Science in Computer Science at the University of Central Florida. He has professional experience at Southern Glazer's Wine & Spirits, Lockheed Martin, and Apple, where he built AI/ML solutions, scalable internal tools, and mission-critical systems. Kevin specializes in full-stack development, AI and machine learning, data engineering, and backend systems.
general_summary: Kevin Muniz is a software engineer and graduate student at the University of Texas at Austin pursuing a Master of Science in Data Science, expected to graduate in December 2027. He previously completed his Bachelor of Science in Computer Science at the University of Central Florida. He currently works at Southern Glazer's Wine and Spirits as an OneTech Data Intern on the AI and Automation team, where he develops AI and ML solutions and internal automation tools. Before that, he worked at Lockheed Martin from September 2024 to May 2026 as both a Full Stack Software Engineer Intern and Systems Engineer Intern, building internal web applications, data transformation tools, and technical documentation for targeting-system programs including the F-35 and F-16. Before Lockheed Martin, Kevin worked at Apple through the College Program as a Technical Specialist from March 2023 to September 2024, managing 75 or more weekly technical cases with a 92% satisfaction rate. Kevin is skilled in Python, Java, JavaScript, C#, Swift, SQL, and C. He builds with React, Node.js, Express, Next.js, FastAPI, Spring Boot, and more. He is experienced with Docker, AWS, PostgreSQL, MongoDB, pgvector, Databricks, and machine learning tools like PyTorch, TensorFlow, and Scikit-learn. His projects include LEMA a cross-platform energy monitoring app, and a secure payment network with ML-based fraud detection. Kevin is outgoing, collaborative, and passionate about fitness, snowboarding, hiking, cooking, traveling, and music. He is seeking full-time software engineering or data roles where he can grow alongside a great team.
work_history_summary: Kevin has worked at three companies: Southern Glazer's Wine and Spirits, Lockheed Martin, and Apple. He is currently working at Southern Glazer's Wine and Spirits as an OneTech Data Intern on the AI and Automation team, starting in May 2026. Before that, he worked at Lockheed Martin from September 2024 to May 2026, holding two roles: Full Stack Software Engineer Intern and Systems Engineer Intern. Before Lockheed Martin, he worked at Apple from March 2023 to September 2024 as a Technical Specialist through the Apple College Program. His background spans software engineering, systems engineering, data science, AI and ML, full-stack web development, and technical support.

education.degree: Master of Science in Data Science
Bachelor of Science in Computer Science

experience.role: OneTech Data Intern - AI and Automation
experience.description: Kevin currently works at Southern Glazer's Wine and Spirits as an OneTech Data Intern on the AI and Automation team. Southern Glazer's is the largest wine and spirits distributor in the United States. In this role Kevin develops AI and ML solutions and internal automation tools that improve business workflows, forecasting processes, and enterprise productivity. He collaborates with data engineering and business teams to analyze large datasets, monitor model performance, and support data-driven decision making. He also builds and supports scalable data and analytics workflows including data preprocessing, evaluation metrics, and backtesting for AI-driven initiatives.
impact: Developed AI/ML solutions and internal automation tools that improved business workflows and forecasting
Collaborated with data engineering teams to analyze large datasets and monitor model performance
Built scalable data and analytics workflows including preprocessing, evaluation metrics, and backtesting

experience.role: Full Stack Software Engineer Intern | Systems Engineer Intern
experience.description: Kevin worked at Lockheed Martin from September 2024 to May 2026, holding both Full Stack Software Engineer Intern and Systems Engineer Intern roles. As a Full Stack Software Engineer Intern he developed an internal reporting web application with a searchable data grid, enabling senior stakeholders to view, filter, and manage structured workforce data more efficiently. He also built a Python-based Excel transformation tool to standardize legacy datasets for a new internal data platform, saving employees approximately 30 minutes per sheet by eliminating manual table restructuring. As a Systems Engineer Intern he improved engineering processes by gathering requirements, documenting workflows, and creating onboarding and reference materials for targeting-system programs, reducing new engineer ramp-up time by 25%.
impact: Built an internal reporting web app with a searchable data grid for senior stakeholders
Created a Python-based Excel transformation tool saving ~30 minutes per sheet
Reduced new engineer ramp-up time by 25% through documentation and onboarding materials
Worked on targeting systems programs at Lockheed Martin

experience.description: Kevin worked at Apple through the Apple College Program as a Technical Specialist. He resolved user cases in Apple's CORE system, managing 75 or more weekly issues and maintaining a 92% customer satisfaction rate. He created and maintained detailed technical case notes for senior engineers, improving issue traceability. He also provided mentorship and career guidance to peers, contributing to multiple students earning Apple internships.
impact: Managed 75+ weekly cases with a 92% customer satisfaction rate
Improved issue traceability through structured technical case notes for senior engineers
Mentored peers, resulting in multiple students earning Apple internships

projects.name: Secure Payment Network
projects.description: Kevin built a distributed payment platform using Java, Spring Boot, Python, PostgreSQL, JWT, Scikit-learn, and Docker. The system has 22 REST endpoints covering authentication, two-factor verification, wallet operations, transaction history, and audit workflows. Kevin secured the financial workflows with short-lived JWTs, bcrypt hashing, endpoint-specific rate limits, TOTP and email verification, recovery codes, and PostgreSQL-backed idempotency keys to prevent duplicate transaction execution. He also integrated a machine learning-based fraud detection service using a Random Forest model trained on 10,000 synthetic transactions, scoring withdrawals and transfers in real time using 6 behavioral features and audit-logging high-risk activity.
details: 22 REST endpoints covering authentication, two-factor verification, wallet operations, transaction history, and audit workflows
Secured with short-lived JWTs, bcrypt hashing, endpoint-specific rate limits, TOTP/email verification, and recovery codes
PostgreSQL-backed idempotency keys to prevent duplicate transaction execution
Random Forest fraud detection model trained on 10,000 synthetic transactions using 6 behavioral features
Real-time scoring of withdrawals and transfers with audit logging for high-risk activity

projects.name: Lightweight Energy Meter Aggregator (LEMA)
projects.description: LEMA is a cross-platform mobile app Kevin built to cut facility energy monitoring costs by up to 80%, enabling scalable deployment for buildings over 5,000 square feet without requiring $50k to $100k enterprise systems. Kevin implemented LAN-based meter polling with offline data caching, achieving 95% or more uptime and full functionality without internet access during connectivity interruptions. He also developed analytics dashboards and CSV, XLSX, and PDF export workflows to streamline energy reporting, reduce manual spreadsheet work, and improve visibility into facility usage trends.
details: Reduces facility energy monitoring costs by up to 80% compared to $50k–$100k enterprise systems
LAN-based meter polling with offline data caching achieving 95%+ uptime without internet connectivity
Analytics dashboards and CSV/XLSX/PDF export workflows for energy reporting
Scalable deployment for buildings over 5,000 square feet

leadership.description: Kevin served as Treasurer of Theta Tau Professional Engineering Fraternity at UCF. He optimized the fraternity budget by conducting a full audit of recurring expenses, cutting unnecessary costs by 25% and strategically reallocating resources toward high-impact academic and professional events. He generated comprehensive monthly financial reports for the executive board with clear visual breakdowns and comparative year-over-year analyses. He also designed and led a weekly financial literacy program for fraternity members covering budgeting, credit management, investing basics, and post-graduate financial planning.
impact: Cut unnecessary costs by 25% through a full financial audit and budget optimization
Delivered monthly financial reports with visual breakdowns and year-over-year comparisons to the executive board
Designed and led a weekly financial literacy program for fraternity members

personality.about: Kevin is naturally outgoing and enjoys talking with people about just about anything, whether it is work, ideas, or everyday life. He loves collaborating and bouncing ideas around to create things that can actually be built and brought to life. While he likes to joke around and keep things fun, he is also able to switch gears quickly and stay focused when it is time to get serious and execute.
hobbies: Kevin is passionate about fitness and enjoys working out regularly to maintain a disciplined and healthy lifestyle. He lifts and runs every day.
He enjoys snowboarding, hiking, and almost any outdoor activity.
Kevin enjoys cooking for others whenever he can. It is one of the ways he shows that he cares.
interests_extended: Kevin is really interested in listening to all kinds of music but his favorite genres are house, rock and indie pop.
He enjoys traveling a lot. Paris, France is his current favorite place he has visited. His dream destination is Japan.
favorite_foods: Kevin enjoys high-protein meals that support his fitness goals, including chicken, steak, and rice-based dishes.
He likes trying new foods and restaurants, especially meals that balance flavor and nutrition.
His favorite foods are sushi and Italian and Latin food.
fun_facts: Kevin has been skydiving. It was really scary but so much more fun.
The only food Kevin does not like is olives.
Kevin is the second engineer in his family.

goals: Kevin is seeking full-time roles in software engineering or data science where he can grow and become the best engineer he can be while working alongside an amazing team. He is passionate about building things that matter and wants to be at a company that feels like family.
